page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 architecture. It features 64 vCPUs derived from 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieved a top-tier rating in Geekbench multi-core and single-core tests, alongside top-tier performance in software compilation and image processing. Memory bandwidth tests show strong read-only performance at 5116.7 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 196.96 GB/sec for large blocks, though large block write bandwidth is average at 82.79 GB/sec. The CPU features advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, which support strong performance in cryptographic operations and LLM inference, such as prompt processing for a 7B model at 303.91 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the c8i-flex.16xlarge offers a high-density compute profile with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient choice for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage. The lack of bundled local storage means all storage must be network-attached, which is a key tradeoff to consider for I/O-heavy applications.

This instance is highly suited for compute-intensive tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, image processing, and medium-scale LLM inference. It is also well-suited for database operations and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in software compilation, image processing, and static web serving, alongside strong multi-core CPU capabilities and memory read bandwidth. With advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, this instance is highly efficient for compute-heavy workloads, including medium-scale LLM inference, multi-threaded decompression, and high-throughput web serving, offering a balanced trade-off between high compute density and memory capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier performance for software compilation and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 64 vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding compilation, web serving, and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated computing
Delivers 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and LLM inference
